title: Mimulus sp. CTAB Low Salt Nucleic Acid Prep
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b7tu9","text":"This protocol is an adaptation of Xin \u0026 Chen (2012) and Arseneau et al. (2017). From the high salt suspension step, users can follow Xin \u0026 Chen (2012) protocol to completion for quick clean-up in a 96-well format. If there is no access to or desire for bead-based cleanup, users can follow the protocol outlined by Arseneau et al. (2017) for the DNA pellet and washing steps. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":65,"length":6},{"style":"italic","offset":324,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b8vsa","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"14pam","text":"It is suggested for use with less than ideal samples (mature fresh leaf with high secondary metabolites, field collection silica dried young leaves, oven-dried leaves). Young Mimulus leaf material generally passes in-house QC using a high salt + CTAB method. I recommend starting with Yuan \u0026 Sweigart (2019) or Fishman (2020) Mimulus protocols on Protocol.io and then trying this protocol for challenging samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cb8cq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fgj65","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"42qp2","text":"This protocol is designed for individual tube format. Users can comfortably complete 96 samples in 3 days using a stacked method (Day 1 Extract, Day 2 Extract/Purify, Day 3 Purify/Quantify). 96 samples are possible in a 2-day format.  The volumes are adapted to make this process efficient, utilizing individual tubes (the use of a combi tip during washes and a larger aqueous phase to draw from during organic phase separation).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9rekn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4rpto","text":"Populations with higher trichome density or purple hue (pigmentation) in leaves were observed to be more challenging to extract cleanly using a standard CTAB protocol in my hands. This protocol was able to handle these samples, along with 2-year-old oven-dried mature leaf tissue. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ca1k2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ra3a","text":"Modifications:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a5ds5","text":"CTAB-DNA complex incubation time is increased to 2 hours, including a cool-down step meant to flocculate the complexes. This can be shortened to 1 hour, depending on your sample. Once the solution begins to precipitate CTAB-DNA complexes, it is fine to begin the spin. High-yielding samples are usually ready in 1 hour of incubation. If the sample fails to precipitate after 2 hours, spin anyway, as a small pellet will likely form. In this case, reduce the final elution volume to 35 uL. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ectu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"98ful","text":"CTAB-DNA centrifugation is increased (3k rcf → 6k rcf) to stabilize the pellet during decanting (pour-off).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"amvnu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9v3vb","text":"DNA-CTAB precipitation centrifugation has increased (3k rcf → 10k rcf) before the high salt suspension for higher yields without sacrificing purity. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6s38a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4rvm9","text":"Two rounds of desalting are used before the final elution. Using one wash yields 260/230 ~ 1.6-2.0, while two washes are generally \u003e2.0. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4n16r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b77k3","text":"Average nanodrop 260/280 1.9 + 0.16 , 260/230 2.16 + 0.3 across all 3 tissue types used n = 690.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":29,"length":1},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":51,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Flash freeze in liquid nitrogen and load into tube mix mill grind plates chilled to -20 °C Mix Mill (MM 300) (Mix Mill, Retsch, 23392, https://americanlaboratorytrading.com/lab-equipment-products/qiagen-retsch-mm300-tissuelyser-lab-vibration-mill-mixer_11444 )
Grind 00:00:45 30/sec and gently knock tissue until free in the tube. Check how well the tissue is ground (should still be frozen). Ground & collected Mimulus leaf
If needed, flash freeze again and flip the orientation of grind plate (tubes closest to the machine to the further position away from the matching), repeat grind until well milled
When ready to extract, spin on a short cycle [ 2-3 sec to ~ 2500 rcf (g) ] to collect tissue from the cap.
Store at -20 °C or proceed directly to extraction. I prefer to load & grind the samples the day before extraction.

For 48 samples, prepare 40 mL CTAB Extraction Buffer + 40 µL Beta-mercaptoethanol (0.1%) + 100 µL Proteinase K (0.1mg/mL) under the fume hood and warm to 60 °C in a waterbath. b-mercaptoethanol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #M3148-25ML Ambion Proteinase K Thermofisher Catalog #AM2546

Under the fume hood, dispense 750 µL warmed CTAB buffer solution (w/ B-mercap) per sample using a p1000 pipette, changing the tip every 6-8 samples or if bubble forms in the tip Optional: Combitip 25mL step level 1.5 Eppendorf Repeater M4 When dispensing CTAB there are few things to note. 1. I like to 'prime' the pipette by drawing the solution up and down once using the first stop of the pipette, then drawing up the volume a second time. This is only the first time you use a fresh tip. 2. Do not go to the second stop on the pipette (air expel) if you are planning to reuse the tip. Doing so will create bubbles that can be sucked into the pipette body. For inexperienced users, consider starting with filter tips to protect the pipette until comfortable. 3. Warmed solutions can often be overdrawn (think of the effect of temperature on meniscus shape and height-of-rise). Pay attention to make sure the volume is not 'creeping' up the pipette if reusing the tip (generally I found every 6-8 samples needed a new tip for accuracy). .
Immediately load and spin samples 3000 rcf, °C, 15 minutes
Vortex samples under the fume hood for 30-60 seconds or until the tissue is dispersed in the buffer.
Incubate in a water bath 60 °C, 60 min , inverting several times gently every 20 minutes to ensure good contact between buffer and tissue. Let tubes cool in the fume hood for 5 minutes
Add equal volume 750 µL Chloroform: Isoamyl alcohol 24:1 to 24 samples. The other 24 samples can remain at room temperature in the fume hood until ready.
Load tissue loosely into a 2.0mL tube with 2x 3.2 mm chrome steel beads (Biospec, cat. 11079132c) 50 mg, dried tissue or 100 mg, fresh leaf

Close tubes, place a paper towel, and then a tube block on top. Shake samples by hand (5-7/sec) between the two blocks for 00:00:20 under the fume hood. Preferred for speed of loading to centrifuge and keeping chloroform under the hood.
Alternatively, return samples to mix mill 00:00:20 at 7/s

Transfer 500 µL into the 1.5 mL tube containing CTAB Dilution Buffer using a p1000 pipette. Mix 1 -2 times by pipette. If interphase is disturbed, recentrifuge sample 3000 rcf, °C, 3 minutes . 2 mL tube with three phases separated out after centrifuging and before transferring 500 ul of the supernatant (top aqueous layer) to the 1.5 mL tube containing CTAB dilution buffer. This sample is from desiccant dried young leaf tissue. Place the tip just below the surface of the top aqueous phase, slowly moving down the tube while drawing up the solution. There is enough aqueous phase (~650 - 750ul ) that no interphase should be disturbed when using a single draw. If air bubbles occur, dispense the volume into the 1.5 ml tube, and then draw up another small portion to complete the volume to the 1.5 ml mark. Top phase added to CTAB Dilution Buffer. Left: oven-dried young leaf, center: fresh mature leaf, right: fresh young leaf. All three resulted in clean DNA with sufficient yield.

In the first 10 minutes, dispense 1.5 mL tubes with 1000 µL CTAB Dilution Buffer NaCl molarity of CTAB extraction buffer is 1.2M, diluting reduces molarity to < 0.5M and precipitates DNA-CTAB complexes leaving polysaccharides in solution (Murray & Thompson 1980; Xin & Chen 2012)
For each sample label: 1x 2.0 mL tube with 2x 3.2 mm chrome steel beads (Biospec, cat. 11079132c) 1x 1.5 mL tube

With the next 24 samples go to step #10 and repeat. OPTIONAL: I like to begin the next 24 samples at the 2 minutes left mark on the spin cycle. The centrifuge is done by the time I finish dispensing chloroform. I unload the 1st batch, then shake and load the 2nd batch. During the 2nd batch spin, I transfer the top phase of the 1st batch.
Incubate samples in a water bath or heat block 65 °C, 60 min

Place samples on shaker 150 rpm, Room temperature, 1 hour or until the solution looks saturated in small 'crystals'. For most extractions, the solution will look saturated. If DNA concentrations are high enough, these 'crystals' will flocculate into long strands and drop out leaving the solution clear with DNA-CTAB 'clumps' at the bottom. These yields tend to be >2.5-3 ug. This solution has yielded up to 7.5 ug. CTAB Precipitation Post Shaking. Left: "crystal" saturated sample (average appearance is like a hazy IPA) Right: formation of flocculate "strands" forming, note larger 'crystal' size above the aggregate.
Spin samples 6000 rcf, Room temperature, 15 minutes
Pour off supernatant gently and completely. If pellet detaches, stop pouring and respin for 5 min before finishing pour. CTAB-DNA pellets. Left: oven-dried young leaf, Right: fresh mature leaf

Add 500 µL Wash Buffer and invert several times until pellet detaches from the wall and begins to dissolve. For faster dispensing, a 25 mL Combitip with step level 1 (500 uL) will fill 48x samples in under 3 minutes This step cleans up contamination from the DNA-CTAB complexes
Put on shaker 150 rpm, Room temperature, 30 minutes
Spin tubes 12000 rcf, Room temperature, 15 minutes Washed CTAB-DNA pellet. Left: dried leaf, Center: fresh mature leaf, Right : fresh young leaf

Gently suspend pellet in 100 µL High Salt TE (1M) + 50 ug/mL final concentration RNASE (0.25 uL/100 uL) Pellet can be suspended gently by pipette, or on shaker RT, 150 RPM, 30 minutes. This step disassociates CTAB from DNA by introducing NaCl > 0.5M, along with degrading RNA. For 48 samples, prepare 5mL TE Buffer 1M NaCl + 12.5 uL Purelink RNASE

Incubate either 37 °C, 60 min and store 4 °C, overnight OR Room temperature, overnight This is usually my stopping point for the day. Another user reported good success with 37C for 1 hour and longer-term storage at -20C. From this point, can either follow either alcohol precipitation and desalting (Arseneau), or use of paramagnetic beads (Xin and Chen). Personal preference is to follow Xin and Chen if Suspension G beads are available.
Pour off supernatant gently and completely. Dab tubes on an autoclaved paper towel to remove trace liquid If time-constrained, pipette off remaining traces of wash buffer using p200 pipette
Dry tubes open 37 °C 15-20 min or until tubes are just dry. Do not over-dry tubes, it will become difficult to resuspend the pellet and can shear DNA.
Add 500 uL 90% ETOH (final concentration of 75%) For faster dispensing, a 25 mL Combitip with step level 1 (500 uL) will fill 48x samples in under 3 minutes
Invert several times and incubate Room temperature, 60 min
Spin 16000 rcf, Room temperature, 20 minutes
Pour off supernatant slowly and completely.
Desalt the pellet using 500 uL Wash Buffer, flick tube to suspend pellet briefly
Spin 16000 rcf, Room temperature, 5 minutes
Pour off supernatant slowly and completely.
If DNA 260/230 of 1.6-1.8 with slightly higher yield is acceptable continue to step 33, else go to step #29 and repeat once for DNA 260/230 > 2.0 (Genome BC/Quebec QC standard).
Dry tubes open at 37 °C, 15-20 min
Add 55 uL low TE ( Genome Quebec) or 10mM Tris-Hcl pH 8.0 Genome Quebec TE: 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 0.1 mM EDTA Standard TE (Cold Harbour Springs): 10 mM Tris-HCL pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA
Incubate 150 rpm, 37 °C, 30 minutes
Mix samples by pipette (gently!) and transfer 54 µL to a PCR plate
Access purity of the sample using 2 µL on a Nanodrop1000/2000

Quantify 2 µL sample using broad spectrum Qubit kit ug/mL can be converted to ng/uL by multiplying *10 for 2 uL volume, (i.e 0.280 ug/ml = 28.0 ng/uL and 1.19 ug/mL = 119 ng/uL) This saves clicking calculate stock concentration everytime between reading samples.

Optional: run 3-5 uL sample on 1.0% agarose gel in TAE buffer, 100V 30 min to check for average molecular weight, and sample integrity. 1% agarose TAE buffer electrophoresis gel. 100V 30 min. Left 2 lanes: fresh mature leaf, Center: Generuler 1kb plus, Right: 2 year old oven dried leaf
Transfer the 100 µL high salt TE DNA solution to 96-well PCR plate, one sample per well. set pipette to 110 µL

Add 5 µL MagAttract suspension G solution to each well using a multichannel pipette. If using a fresh vial of MagAttract, vortex for 3 min before dispensing If using a previously opened vial of MagAttract, vortex for 1 min before using
Add 120 µL 100% ethanol to each well using multichannel pipette.

Cover the 96-well PCR plate tightly with a silicone plate sealing mat and mix gently in your hands (to allow the DNA to adhere to the beads). Place a block over the silicon mat to seal, and invert several times to suspend the beads in solution (a flick of the wrist as you invert the plate helps mix the beads in the ethanol)
Place the DNA plate on Magnet B to hold the MagAttract beads and then pour off the ethanol solution. Pour off waste into waste beaker or sink while still on magnet rack
Wash the beads three times with 200 µL washing buffer and, after the final wash, air-dry the beads for 00:10:00 at RT. complete the washes and pouring off of wash buffer on the magnetic plate
Add 55 µL TE to each sample well to re-suspend DNA. Re-suspend DNA off the magnetic plate

Incubate at 60 °C 00:05:00 and mix gently (to allow the bound DNA release into TE solution). 1. Put silicon sealing mat back on 2. Incubate at 60 °C in a PCR thermocycler 3. After thermocyler is finished, mix samples off the magnetic rack gently with the multichannel pipette
Place the plate on a magnetic plate and wait 00:02:00 minutes before transferring 54 µL of the elution buffer to a new 96-well plate.
Check the purity of the sample using 2 µL on a Nanodrop1000/2000
Quantify 2 µL sample using broad spectrum Qubit kit

Optional: run a 3-5 μL sample on a 1.0% agarose gel in TAE buffer at 100V for 30 min to check for molecular weight distribution and sample integrity. 1% agarose TAE buffer electrophoresis gel. 100V 30 min. Left 2 lanes: fresh mature leaf, Center: Generuler 1kb plus, Right: 2 year old oven dried leaf
creator: Dylan Moxley
